title: Questions About Intelligence Agencies and Uranium One
summary: Drop #3 continued the question-based format and referenced intelligence officials, Uranium One, and media influence narratives.
raw_text:
Who controls the NSA?
Who controls the FBI?
Do you believe in coincidences?
Who had access to HRC's emails?
Why is this relevant?
What agency has the power to conduct operations and gather intel over our domestic agencies?
Why was the NSA created?
Why is this relevant?
Who was Admiral Rogers?
What did Admiral Rogers do upon learning of the political surveillance and illegal collection of data?
How did he violate standard procedures?
Why is this relevant?
What was the Russian dossier?
Who paid for it?
Why is this relevant?
What is Uranium One?
Why is this relevant?
Q

lines.fact: Uranium One was a uranium mining company whose acquisition by Rosatom was approved by U.S. agencies in 2010.
lines.theory: Q followers interpreted Uranium One as evidence of corruption involving the Clinton Foundation and Russian interests.
lines.context: Multiple investigations reviewed the transaction; no criminal charges against Hillary Clinton resulted from the deal.
lines.questions: Did any verified evidence show direct bribery connected to the approval process?

lines.fact: Admiral Mike Rogers served as Director of the NSA and Commander of U.S. Cyber Command.
lines.theory: Some followers believed Rogers warned Donald Trump about surveillance activities.
lines.context: Rogers became a recurring figure in early Q narratives about intelligence reform.
lines.questions: What documented actions by Rogers directly relate to the claims implied here?
